artifact_warning: Manuscript-candidate package-native output; geometry/noise assumptions are closer to V24 but algorithm fidelity and final human signoff are still required before TAES submission.

coarse_jcls.bias_note: Clock offsets are estimated jointly with UE positions using one measurement epoch.
coarse_jcls.label: Coarse JCLS
coarse_jcls.satellite_clocks: non-reference satellite clocks estimated; first satellite fixed
coarse_jcls.state_estimated: full V24 theta: UE positions, UE clocks, non-reference satellite clocks

refined_jcls.bias_note: Uses the coarse JCLS solution as initialization. Manuscript-candidate mode applies dynamic SCI/SFI information updates with explicit F, Q, and Pi; legacy diagnostic mode fuses repeated static-geometry epochs.
refined_jcls.label: Refined JCLS, 0.5 s
refined_jcls.satellite_clocks: non-reference satellite clocks estimated; first satellite fixed
refined_jcls.state_estimated: full V24 theta; implementation selected by estimator_mode metadata

without_cooperation.bias_note: This conventional TOA baseline is intentionally vulnerable to residual UE and satellite clock offsets because it does not estimate clock states.
without_cooperation.label: Without cooperation

estimator_metadata.diagnostic_static_repeated_fusion: Legacy package diagnostic mode: coarse solution is used to initialize a static full-theta least-squares fit over repeated independent epochs.

v24_three_stage_dynamic.step1: weighted GN UE-only DL localization with clock states fixed to zero
v24_three_stage_dynamic.step2: weighted LM over the full gauged V24 theta vector
